question: Define a C++ `struct Book` with members `std::string title`, `std::string author`, and `int publicationYear`. In `main`, create an array of 3 `Book` structs, populate their details, and then iterate through the array to print the details of each book.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1:
  Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
  Author: Douglas Adams
  Year: 

Book 2:
  Title: Pride and Prejudice
  Author: Jane Austen
  Year: 

Book 3:
  Title: 1984
  Author: George Orwell
  Year:

errors.message: The `publicationYear` (an integer) is explicitly cast to `char` during printing, which misinterprets its semantic meaning. It should be printed as an integer to represent the year correctly.
